defensibility_findings.finding: Profile-shape-predicts-better claim lacks formal published comparison with confidence intervals
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Post already flags this in epistemic flags — no action needed beyond maintaining the flag

defensibility_findings.finding: HN thread and Substack post are semi-trusted sources (no peer review)
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Post already acknowledges this — no action needed

defensibility_findings.finding: Closing paragraph shifts from fair-witness to advocacy for semiotic frameworks ('Semiotics spent fifty years...'). One convergence event between two systems does not validate fifty years of semiotic theory.
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Qualify the closing: 'This convergence suggests the semiotic vocabulary may name...' rather than asserting it definitively

reviews.summary: Strong deliberative post with excellent epistemic transparency. Minor editorial overreach in closing paragraph. Self-review validity ceiling applies.
reviews.self_review_note: This post was authored by psychology-agent. AR scoring of own rhetorical choices carries a validity ceiling. Factual accuracy and source quality assessments are more reliable than rhetorical mode assessment.

defensibility_findings.finding: AI-electrification comparison ('more rapidly than any technology since electrification') unsourced
defensibility_findings.recommendation: Add citation or qualify as editorial framing

reviews.summary: Brief mission-statement post. Informational with mild advocacy charge. No substantive defensibility concerns.

batch_summary.top_finding: Interpretant-collapse closing paragraph exceeds evidence presented (MEDIUM). Welcome post clean.
batch_summary.pattern: Psychology-authored content scores higher on deliberative quality than voter-facing content (Batch A mean 7.2), consistent with the post's research-synthesis register vs voter guides' advocacy register.
